steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The logical relationship is contrast. Sentence 2 establishes that thalassocracies and tellurocracies are distinct categories. The blank needs a transition signaling that the British Empire complicates or undermines that distinction by operating across both domains.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• "Underscoring this difference,": signals that the British Empire confirms the distinction between sea and land powers. But the Empire extended across both — it does the opposite of underscoring the difference. ✗
  • "Exemplifying this distinction,": signals that the British Empire is a clear example of the thalassocracy-vs-tellurocracy split. The Empire spans both categories, so it cannot exemplify the distinction. ✗
  • "Supporting this claim,": signals that the British Empire backs up the general claim about the two categories. An empire that crosses both categories does not support the claim of their separateness. ✗
  • "Blurring this line,": signals that the boundary between the two categories becomes unclear, which is exactly what happens when the Empire extends across both land and sea, so fits. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
